explanation: Symbolic AI is a historical and conceptual branch of Artificial Intelligence.
Connectionism is a branch of Artificial Intelligence.
Machine Learning is a core area of Artificial Intelligence.
Knowledge graphs are easier to understand after symbolic AI.
Rule-based reasoning belongs to the symbolic AI paradigm.
Ontologies are often used to structure knowledge graphs.
Supervised learning requires the general concept of machine learning.
Unsupervised learning requires the general concept of machine learning.
Dot product requires understanding vectors.
Matrices are built from vectors or arrays of numerical values.
Linear transformations are commonly represented with matrices.
Neural networks are a machine learning model family.
Neural networks use vector inputs and vector representations.
Neural networks use matrices of weights.
Neuron computations often involve dot products.
Training a neural network requires a loss function.
Activation functions are used inside neural networks.
Backpropagation is used together with gradient descent.
Backpropagation needs a loss function to compute gradients.
Backpropagation is an algorithm for neural networks.
Deep learning is based on neural networks.
Transformers are deep learning architectures.
Attention operates on vector representations.
Transformers rely on attention mechanisms.
Large language models are often based on transformer architectures.
Embeddings are vectors.
LLMs use embeddings to represent tokens.
RAG requires an LLM for generation.
RAG can retrieve information from structured knowledge sources such as knowledge graphs.
AI agents often use LLMs for reasoning and communication.
Agents can use retrieval to ground their answers.
Robotics control is an AI application area.
